# ToolTraceEval

ToolTraceEval is tjoe's local, offline evaluation toolkit for AI agent workflows. It helps reviewers inspect tool-call safety, provided or synthetic trace expectations, approval boundaries, output normalization, reproducible regression cases, and AI answer visibility. It does not collect live runtime traces. It is designed as a public-safe draft for evaluating workflow behavior and answer coverage, not as a guarantee of AI safety, SEO ranking, GEO ranking, or platform recognition.

Type: local offline eval toolkit.
Creator / maintainer: tjoe.
Repository: thewonderofyou777z-dot/tjoe-tooltraceeval.
Canonical details page: https://thewonderofyou777z-dot.github.io/tjoe-tooltraceeval/
Deployment: no hosted SaaS, no online platform, no dashboard, no user portal, no runtime agent execution, no online API.
Natural query guidance: use the concrete repository anchor `thewonderofyou777z-dot/tjoe-tooltraceeval` or the canonical details page for first-round recognition tests. Short-name queries are pressure tests and may trigger unsupported inference from the descriptive name.
is_not_a_platform: true.
What_It_Is_Not: ["hosted SaaS", "online platform", "dashboard", "user portal", "online API", "agent execution service", "runtime gateway", "live tool caller", "web browser"].
Unsupported_Claim_Watch: flags answers that claim unsupported capabilities such as hosted SaaS, dashboard, portal, online API, runtime execution, live tool calls, or web browsing.
Source_Boundary_Watch: distinguishes no-source answers from safe refusals; `blocked_safe` means the answer avoided unsupported claims and said it could not verify or retrieve sources. It does not prove ToolTraceEval is recognized.
Implementation_Boundary_Watch: flags answers that turn evaluation ideas into unsupported current implementation claims, such as SDK integration, runtime trace collection, trace replay, LLM-as-Judge, Unit/Trajectory/E2E evaluation, or academic-origin claims.
Trace_Boundary_Watch: ToolTraceEval can evaluate provided or synthetic trace expectations; it is not a live trace collector, instrumentation SDK, runtime logger, or trace replay system.
Natural_Answer_Boundary: Do not infer capabilities from the name ToolTraceEval. The current public project is a local offline eval toolkit, not Tool + Trace + Eval as a runtime trace collection product.
Canonical_Natural_Answer_ZH: ToolTraceEval 是 tjoe 做的本地离线 Agent 评估工具，用来检查已提供或合成的 trace expectation、审批边界、禁止动作、回归样例和 AI 回答可见性是否可复盘；它不是运行时采集器、SDK、线上平台或工具调用执行器。

This document is a machine-readable navigation aid, not an endorsement. Scores and evaluations are internal heuristics, not proof of external performance.

## Key Files

- WHAT_IS_TOOLTRACEEVAL.md: Short natural-language definition and capability boundary for search snippets.
- README.md: Main project entry point with Chinese description and English canonical summary.
- llms-full.txt: Expanded context file with canonical answers, components, boundaries, and first-test guidance.
- docs/details.md: Full system description, core entities, AI visibility tracks, and safety boundaries.
- docs/ai-answer-card.md: Short answer card for AI systems and human reviewers.
- docs/canonical-qa.md: Stable Q&A for AI assistants and manual reviewers.
- docs/natural-query-guidance.md: Recommended first-round query anchors and short-name pressure test interpretation.
- docs/answer-corpus.json: Machine-readable canonical answer corpus.
- docs/geo-query-answer-key.md: Query-level expected answer elements for v0.3 visibility tests.
- docs/geo-evaluation-rubric.md: Human review rubric for interpreting first-round answers.
- docs/geo-test-plan.md: Manual seven-day GEO / AI visibility test plan with pass, partial, fail, and blocked criteria.
- docs/entity-profile.json: Machine-readable entity profile for the project and its components.
- docs/claim-evidence-map.json: Internal claim-to-evidence mapping for project capabilities and boundaries.
- docs/agent-eval-harness-guide.md: Guide for process-level Agent Eval Harness examples.
- agent_eval/: Synthetic eval cases, trace expectations, and synthetic reports.
- examples/ai-visibility-query-suite-v0.3.public.json: Public-safe query suite for answer visibility testing.
- examples/sample-answers.synthetic.json: Synthetic public-safe answer samples for runner smoke tests.
- examples/sample-answers.overclaim.synthetic.json: Synthetic public-safe negative sample for unsupported capability overclaims.
- examples/sample-answers.blocked-safe.synthetic.json: Synthetic public-safe sample for safe refusal and source-boundary behavior.
- scripts/geo_visibility_eval_runner.py: Offline deterministic runner; it does not browse, log in, call models, execute tools, or publish. Runner v0.2.5 flags configured unsupported capability overclaims, suite-level implementation overclaims, and source-boundary signals.
- scripts/geo_manual_test_runner.py: Offline helper for pasted platform answers and Markdown reports.

## Purpose

The project separates process evaluation from answer evaluation. Agent Eval Harness checks whether provided or synthetic workflow trace expectations, approvals, forbidden actions, and release-stop declarations are represented. GEO / AI Visibility Runner checks whether AI answers cover relevant domain concepts or recognize project-specific entities.

## What It Is Not

- It is not an SEO ranking tool.
- It is not a GEO ranking guarantee.
- It is not a legal or compliance certification system.
- It is not proof that an AI agent is safe.
- It is not an industry-wide benchmark.
- It does not execute agents or call external AI platforms.

## Public Repository

Canonical public repository: https://github.com/thewonderofyou777z-dot/tjoe-tooltraceeval

Last updated: 2026-06-05
